rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF DARLENE J FULLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Conservatorship funds (~$140,000) are being held at a bank in Ohio, over which this court does not have jurisdiction. In addition, the cash verification does not fully comply with court requirements. 2)Attorney fee request: a.Attorney's request for fees initially included services provided on 10/11/16, 10/31/16, and 11/1/16 totaling $2,490, which had already been requested for and granted in the previous accounting period. Attorney has corrected the request and apologizes for the mistake. b.Almost $3,000 was charged to file an amended petition. Court to review explanation provided in 12/24/18 declaration and in additional declaration filed on 2/26/19. c.Attorney provided services regarding preparation of the 2nd accounting, which he estimates that ½ of the time was devoted to verifying that the correct information was transferred by his paralegal from bank statements to Judicial Council Forms, which does not appear to be legal services. Attorney also alleges that no paralegal services were charged.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GAUALOFA M. CASEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: continued to april 29, 2019. there is a post-deceased heir. petitioner must file a second declaration stating not just whether a probate was opened, but whether the post-deceased heir died intestate or testate, and if testate, she must identify the beneficiaries under the will.
